page.title: Galeon 500 Fly vs Azimut 58: Marbella Charter Compared

data.meta_description: Compare the Galeon 500 Fly vs Azimut 58 for your Marbella charter. We break down size, capacity, and price so you can choose the right boat for your day on the Costa del Sol.
data.summary: A detailed comparison of the Galeon 500 Fly and Azimut 58 for charter in Marbella, covering dimensions, passenger capacity, amenities, and pricing. Helps readers decide which flybridge yacht suits their group and budget.
key_facts: Galeon 500 Fly: 15.5m, 12 pax, flybridge, departs Marbella Marina.
Azimut 58 Flybridge: 17.6m, 12 pax, flybridge, departs Puerto Banús.
Azimut 58 is 2.1m longer, offering more deck space and cabin volume.
Both boats carry 12 passengers – ideal for groups up to a dozen.
Galeon 500 Fly pricing: on request via WhatsApp (ask for a quote).
Both charters include skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, and 21% IVA.
Azimut 58 departs from Puerto Banús; Galeon 500 Fly from Marbella Marina.

faq.q: Which boat is bigger, the Galeon 500 Fly or the Azimut 58?
faq.a: The Azimut 58 is larger at 17.6m compared to the Galeon 500 Fly's 15.5m. That extra 2.1m translates to more deck space, a larger flybridge, and additional cabin volume below deck.

faq.q: Can I charter the Galeon 500 Fly from Puerto Banús?
faq.a: No, the Galeon 500 Fly departs from Marbella Marina, while the Azimut 58 departs from Puerto Banús. Both marinas are within 10 minutes of each other by car along the Golden Mile.

faq.q: How many passengers can each boat take?
faq.a: Both the Galeon 500 Fly and the Azimut 58 are certified for 12 passengers. That makes either a great choice for larger groups, family celebrations, or corporate outings.

faq.q: What is included in the charter price?
faq.a: Every charter with us includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). No hidden extras.

faq.q: Does the Azimut 58 come with a jet ski?
faq.a: Yes, the Azimut 58 charter includes a jet ski free of charge for the duration of your trip. The Galeon 500 Fly does not include a jet ski as standard – you can ask about adding one via WhatsApp.

faq.q: What is the price for the Galeon 500 Fly?
faq.a: Pricing for the Galeon 500 Fly is on request. Contact us via WhatsApp for a tailored quote based on your preferred date, duration, and any extras you may want.

faq.q: Which boat is better for a party or celebration?
faq.a: Both are excellent. The Azimut 58 offers a larger flybridge and the bonus of a jet ski, making it ideal for active groups. The Galeon 500 Fly has a more intimate feel – perfect for a relaxed day with close friends.

faq.q: Can I see the Galeon 500 Fly or Azimut 58 before booking?
faq.a: Absolutely. We recommend visiting Puerto Banús or Marbella Marina to view the boats in person. Contact us via WhatsApp to arrange a viewing at your convenience.

data.body_html:
If you're planning a day on the water in Marbella and can't decide between the Galeon 500 Fly and the Azimut 58 Flybridge, you're not alone. Both are premium flybridge yachts that offer space, style, and sun. But they differ in size, departure point, and inclusions. Here's a head-to-head comparison to help you choose the right charter for your group.

Dimensions and Capacity

The Azimut 58 measures 17.6m in length, while the Galeon 500 Fly comes in at 15.5m. That 2.1m difference gives the Azimut more deck space, a larger flybridge, and more generous cabins below. Both boats are certified for 12 passengers, so capacity isn't a deciding factor – it's all about how you want to use the space.

Departure Points: Puerto Banús vs Marbella Marina

The Azimut 58 departs from Puerto Banús, the iconic marina lined with luxury boutiques and restaurants. The Galeon 500 Fly departs from Marbella Marina, a quieter base just east of the Golden Mile. If you're staying in central Marbella, the Galeon is more convenient. If you want to start your day with a stroll through Puerto Banús, go with the Azimut.

Inclusions and Extras

Both charters include a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and 21% IVA. The Azimut 58 sweetens the deal with a jet ski included free for the entire charter – ideal for thrill-seekers. The Galeon 500 Fly does not include a jet ski as standard, but you can ask about adding one via WhatsApp.

Pricing: A Clear Difference

The Azimut 58 is priced at €4,719 for a minimum of 4 hours (jet ski included). The Galeon 500 Fly is pricing on request – contact us via WhatsApp for a personalised quote. If you want a fixed, all-inclusive price with a jet ski, the Azimut is the straightforward choice. If you prefer to negotiate extras or have a smaller budget, the Galeon may be more flexible.

Which Boat for Which Occasion?

Choose the Azimut 58 if you want a larger flybridge, a jet ski, and a departure from Puerto Banús. It's perfect for active groups, families with teenagers, or anyone who wants to combine cruising with watersports. Choose the Galeon 500 Fly if you prefer a more intimate feel, a quieter marina, and the flexibility to customise your charter. Both boats are excellent for sunset cruises, lunch at Cala del Faro, or anchoring off Cabopino.

Explore Our Full Fleet

Still unsure? Browse our complete boat rental Marbella fleet to compare all available yachts. We also have the Azimut 58 Flybridge page with more details, and you can read our Marbella charter blog for tips on choosing the right boat.

Frequently Asked Questions

Which boat is bigger, the Galeon 500 Fly or the Azimut 58?

The Azimut 58 is larger at 17.6m compared to the Galeon 500 Fly's 15.5m. That extra 2.1m translates to more deck space, a larger flybridge, and additional cabin volume below deck.

Can I charter the Galeon 500 Fly from Puerto Banús?

No, the Galeon 500 Fly departs from Marbella Marina, while the Azimut 58 departs from Puerto Banús. Both marinas are within 10 minutes of each other by car along the Golden Mile.

How many passengers can each boat take?

Both the Galeon 500 Fly and the Azimut 58 are certified for 12 passengers. That makes either a great choice for larger groups, family celebrations, or corporate outings.

What is included in the charter price?

Every charter with us includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). No hidden extras.

Does the Azimut 58 come with a jet ski?

Yes, the Azimut 58 charter includes a jet ski free of charge for the duration of your trip. The Galeon 500 Fly does not include a jet ski as standard – you can ask about adding one via WhatsApp.

What is the price for the Galeon 500 Fly?

Pricing for the Galeon 500 Fly is on request. Contact us via WhatsApp for a tailored quote based on your preferred date, duration, and any extras you may want.

Which boat is better for a party or celebration?

Both are excellent. The Azimut 58 offers a larger flybridge and the bonus of a jet ski, making it ideal for active groups. The Galeon 500 Fly has a more intimate feel – perfect for a relaxed day with close friends.

Can I see the Galeon 500 Fly or Azimut 58 before booking?

Absolutely. We recommend visiting Puerto Banús or Marbella Marina to view the boats in person. Contact us via WhatsApp to arrange a viewing at your convenience.
